tech_news.title: Microsoft’s ‘Xbox mode’ is coming to every Windows 11 PC - The Verge
tech_news.description: Starting in April Microsoft is bringing its full-screen Xbox mode to every kind of Windows 11 PC, including laptops, desktops, and tablets.

tech_news.title: Microsoft’s next Xbox, Project Helix, won’t reach alpha until 2027 - The Verge
tech_news.description: Microsoft announced details about Project Helix, its next-generation Xbox, in a presentation at the GDC Festival of Gaming.

tech_news.title: Intel shores up its desktop CPU lineup with boosted Core Ultra 200S Plus chips - Ars Technica
tech_news.description: New CPUs are a bit faster and a bit cheaper than the ones they replace.

tech_news.title: Enjoy New Prizes and Newly Distributed Battle Boxes at Play! Pokémon Stores - Pokemon.com
tech_news.description: Acrylic standees, stickers, and Battle Boxes are coming to local Play! Pokémon Stores starting April 1, 2026.

tech_news.title: New iPhone 18 Pro leaks explore design details, dimensions, more - 9to5Mac
tech_news.description: New iPhone 18 Pro-related posts from Weibo leakers outline what to expect from the product’s design, dimensions, and more.

tech_news.title: Former Apple engineer raises $5M for a note-taking pendant that only records your voice - TechCrunch
tech_news.description: Taya is trying to address privacy concerns around note-taking wearables with its device that can be worn as a pendant and records only the user's voice.

tech_news.title: After using MacBook Neo, it's clear Windows needs to rethink its PC strategy (and fast) - ZDNET
tech_news.description: Apple's new $599 laptop will force Windows PCs and Chromebooks to respond in turn. That's a good thing.

tech_news.title: Apple Just Transformed the CarPlay Experience in iOS 26.4 Beta 4 - Geeky Gadgets
tech_news.description: iOS 26.4 beta 4 adds a CarPlay Music privacy confirmation screen; new emojis like Bigfoot arrive, with small heat reports on some devices.

tech_news.title: Google is optimizing Android's core to make your phone feel faster - Android Authority
tech_news.description: Google is introducing a new optimization trick to make the core of Android run faster by studying how phones are actually used.
